nodes.title: Define the complete light token set
nodes.why: The light theme establishes every semantic token that components will consume later, including contrast and shadow values.
nodes.alternative: Define only page background and text variables, then hard-code component colors.
nodes.tradeoff: A fuller token set is more upfront CSS, but it prevents component rules from becoming theme-specific.

nodes.title: Override the same tokens for OS dark mode
nodes.why: Redefining the same custom properties lets the browser preference switch the whole surface without changing component selectors.
nodes.alternative: Duplicate all component declarations inside the media query.
nodes.tradeoff: Token overrides require discipline in naming, but they keep component styles stable across themes.

nodes.title: Place the manual override after the media query
nodes.why: :where(.theme-dark) adds no specificity, so this rule ties :root and wins by later source order.
nodes.alternative: Use html.theme-dark with higher specificity or place the override before the media query.
nodes.tradeoff: Equal specificity makes the cascade easy to explain, but the rule order becomes part of the contract.

nodes.title: Read page colors from variables
nodes.why: The page-level background and text should respond to whichever token layer wins.
nodes.alternative: Keep body colors as literal values independent of the token system.
nodes.tradeoff: Variable reads are slightly less direct than literals, but they make the theme swap live and centralized.

nodes.title: Keep panel colors and shadow tokenized
nodes.why: The panel consumes surface, text, border, and shadow tokens so all colored presentation changes with the theme.
nodes.alternative: Tokenize colors but leave a literal rgba shadow in the component rule.
nodes.tradeoff: A shadow token adds one more variable, but shadows include color and need theme-specific values too.

nodes.title: Use an explicit accent contrast token
nodes.why: The accent background needs a paired foreground token so button text remains readable in each theme.
nodes.alternative: Assume white text is always readable on the accent color.
nodes.tradeoff: A contrast token is extra naming, but it makes accessibility intent explicit and testable.
